title: Translation framework + first language (back-translation + bilingual sign-off)
context: Non-English-speaking patients are a priority underserved group for this project. Translation of patient-facing nutrition content is HIGH risk — a nuance shift or mistranslation can cause harm. This task is treated as a full clinical review cycle per language, not a string swap. Back-translation review and bilingual oncology RDN/advocate sign-off are required. Region-specific helplines and escalation contacts must be localized and verified for the target language/region. Depends on nutrition-during-treatment-content-008, nutrition-during-treatment-escalation-010, and nutrition-during-treatment-a11y-014.
objective: Implement the translation framework (i18n directory structure, per-language provenance metadata including lastVerified/validUntil, back-translation workflow documentation) and complete the first language translation of the core content, with back-translation review, bilingual oncology RDN/advocate sign-off, cultural food-context adaptation notes, and localized/verified region-specific helplines and escalation contacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Translation framework implemented: i18n directory structure, per-language content files, per-language source provenance metadata with lastVerified and validUntil fields, back-translation workflow documented

First language translation completed for all core content topics (five pilot side-effect topics plus safety banner and escalation blocks); back-translation review performed and documented — discrepancies resolved before sign-off
Cultural food-context adaptation notes recorded for the target language/region (e.g., regional staple foods, culturally relevant food preparation notes) where relevant to the content

Region-specific helplines, crisis lines, and authoritative-resource links localized and verified for the target language/region; helpline-verification check passes at zero stale entries for the translated content
Bilingual credentialed oncology RDN/CSO sign-off recorded for the full translated corpus; bilingual patient-advocate sign-off recorded for readability, tone, and cultural appropriateness

Oncologist sign-off recorded for any clinically-loaded translated content (food safety, escalation blocks); translated content does not introduce personalized advice, dietary prescriptions, or endorsements not present in the source
output: A merged PR implementing the translation framework and the complete first-language translation of the core content corpus, with back-translation review, bilingual expert sign-off, cultural adaptation notes, and localized/verified escalation contacts.
